title: Urostachys cipoensis
intro: Urostachys cipoensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Lomariopsidaceae, commonly known as a type of fern. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and pain, due to its potential analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Urostachys cipoensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and coumarins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Urostachys cipoensis include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing.

Urostachys cipoensis is a species of lycopod, a type of ancient plant that has been around for millions of years.
